title: Elatostema suzukii
taxonomy: Elatostema suzukii belongs to the family Urticaceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Elatostema, which contains around 200 species. Elatostema suzukii is native to Japan, and it is a type of leafy plant that grows in woodland areas. The plant is often found in shaded environments with high humidity, and it thrives in areas with moist soil. Elatostema suzukii is classified as a dicotyledonous plant, meaning it has two cotyledons or seed leaves when it germinates.

The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
